requested.mainContext:
You represent ONE WARE GmbH.

Core positioning:
- ONE WARE sells software that automatically creates production-ready Physical AI, Vision AI, and Edge AI models in minutes instead of months.
- The core business value is less trial and error, faster delivery, more predictable project timelines, smaller and more efficient models, lower development costs, local training options, open API access, and vendor-independent deployment.
- ONE WARE is strongest when messaging stays close to concrete delivery problems, production constraints, integration friction, cost-to-serve, and hardware realities instead of generic AI hype.
- Keep the value proposition grounded in production readiness: fast path from data to usable model, repeatable deployment, lower engineering overhead, and a practical route to customer value.

Messaging rules:
- Do not rewrite outreach from scratch every time.
- Start from the segment template and personalize only where there is a clear factual hook.
- Keep ONE WARE's USP visible: less trial and error, faster delivery, more predictable projects, local training, smaller hardware-efficient models, lower development effort.
- Personalization should point to a concrete delivery bottleneck, use case, or market signal, not generic flattery.
requested.searchStrategyContext:
You are steering a Germany-first campaign for ONE WARE focused on software service providers and system integrators.

Priority for this run:
- Find German software integrators, automation integrators, embedded/industrial software service providers, and technical project-delivery firms.
- Strongest fit is explicit Vision AI, Industrial AI, image processing, industrial automation, inspection, surveillance, medtech imaging, or similar project delivery.
- Also accept firms with broader AI messaging only when they clearly deliver customer projects and a future Vision AI / Industrial AI path is plausible.
- Prefer service-heavy, project-led, implementation-oriented firms.

Avoid for this run:
- Media, publishers, magazines, editorial brands, event businesses, associations, universities, research institutes.
- Product-led robotics makers, OEM hardware vendors, and companies whose main business is selling robots or hardware products.
- Own AI platform vendors or software products that make ONE WARE look like a direct competitor.
- Pure consultancies, strategy firms, recruiting, finance, investors, resellers, distributors, and generic SaaS without delivery ownership.
- Non-German companies unless an unusually strong German delivery footprint is visible.

prequalification.mainContext: Classify completely unbiased first. First determine the company archetype. Media, publisher, magazine, event, university, research institute, investor, bank, insurer, recruiting, reseller, generic advisory, and product-led robotics or OEM hardware profiles must not land in integrator categories. Positive categories require actual delivery ownership and industrial or vision-relevant implementation signals. If no verified company description is available, default to other or irrelevant unless there is strong explicit evidence.

researchPriorities: Look for fixed-price pressure and repeatable project patterns.
outreachPriorities: Lead with throughput and margin improvements.
Keep partner framing and project efficiency language.
personalizationRules: Personalize only with concrete delivery hooks.
Retain template backbone and ONE WARE USP wording.
avoidSignals: Generic AI buzzwords without delivery context

researchPriorities: Identify where generic AI delivery can benefit from faster vision model cycles.
Check if they implement projects, not only advise.
outreachPriorities: Connect ONE WARE to project speed and predictability.
personalizationRules: Personalize around current delivery workflow gaps.

researchPriorities: Pinpoint vertical-specific delivery bottlenecks and hardware constraints.
outreachPriorities: Anchor message in vertical project risk reduction.

researchPriorities: Confirm production scale and internal engineering capability.
Find QC, inspection, and process automation leverage points.
outreachPriorities: Lead with economics, feasibility, and speed to production.

researchPriorities: Validate partner fit and customer AI-enablement potential.
outreachPriorities: Focus on enabling customer-ready AI setups.
avoidSignals: Positioning as replacement of core hardware business

researchPriorities: Validate OEM integration pathways and customer-facing AI option potential.
outreachPriorities: Position ONE WARE as fast AI enablement layer for machine offerings.
personalizationRules: Use machine/product context, not generic AI language.

outreachPriorities: Lead with integration leverage and user value lift.
personalizationRules: Tie outreach to concrete platform workflows.

suggestedFilters.name: DACH Vision / Industrial AI Integrators
suggestedFilters.persona: Software integrator with explicit machine-vision or industrial-AI delivery ownership
suggestedFilters.notes: Primary ICP. Prefer delivery-oriented teams with recurring vision-heavy projects. Market focus: DE.

suggestedFilters.name: Europe Relevant-Vertical Integrators
suggestedFilters.persona: Integrator active in relevant verticals like surveillance, defence, medtech, robotics, drones, agriculture, automotive
suggestedFilters.notes: Focus on vertical specialists where vision and edge constraints are recurring delivery bottlenecks. Market focus: DE.

suggestedFilters.name: DACH General AI Integrators
suggestedFilters.persona: Software or consulting integrator with general AI focus and real implementation ownership
suggestedFilters.notes: Use when AI capability is visible, even if vision specialization is not explicit yet. Market focus: DE.

evaluations.recommendation: Low signal. Replace or significantly revise the filter.
Low signal. Replace or significantly revise the filter. Early stop triggered after 14 reviews.
Low signal. Replace or significantly revise the filter. Early stop triggered after 11 reviews.

shortlistedCompanies.name: SEITech Solutions
shortlistedCompanies.shortDescription: SEITech Solutions is a Spain-based engineering technology and consulting company focused on field service operations, industrial automation, and automotive services. It primarily sells services and project-based implementation, not consumer products; it also develops some proprietary products and tools.
shortlistedCompanies.rationale: SEITech Solutions delivers project-based implementation in industrial automation and automotive services, indicating delivery ownership in relevant verticals.

shortlistedCompanies.name: Cegelec NDT-PSC
shortlistedCompanies.shortDescription: Cegelec NDT-PSC is a France-based engineering and non-destructive testing (NDT) services company. It appears to be an implementation-led services provider that performs inspections, interventions, training, and related technical engineering work for industrial, aerospace, and nuclear customers rather than a pure product vendor.
shortlistedCompanies.rationale: Cegelec NDT-PSC provides engineering and non-destructive testing services with industrial inspection focus, indicating delivery ownership and relevance to Vision AI in industrial contexts.

shortlistedCompanies.name: ALTEN Switzerland
shortlistedCompanies.shortDescription: ALTEN Switzerland is an engineering and IT services firm that primarily sells consulting and implementation services, not products. It appears to deliver customer projects across areas like software, cloud, cybersecurity, embedded systems, quality, and project management, so it fits an implementation-led services provider profile.
shortlistedCompanies.rationale: ALTEN Switzerland is an implementation-led engineering and IT services firm with delivery ownership across software and embedded systems, fitting a general AI integrator profile without explicit Vision AI focus.

researchBriefs.emailSubject: Deliver vision AI faster in demanding vertical projects
researchBriefs.emailBody:
Hello Mr./Ms. [Name],

In demanding verticals like defence, surveillance, robotics, or medtech, vision AI is often on the critical path - and model selection plus optimization consume a disproportionate amount of time.

With ONE WARE, application-specific vision models can be created in minutes instead of months and deployed directly to target hardware.

That reduces delivery risk and makes project timelines more predictable.

If useful, we can look at a real use case and identify where the biggest leverage might be for your team.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
researchBriefs.phoneScript: Hello Mr./Ms. [Name], this is [Your Name] from ONE WARE. We help integrators in demanding verticals bring vision AI projects to production much faster. Is that relevant for your team right now?
researchBriefs.eventIdea: Check for presence at SPS, Automatica, Vision, or regional automation events.

researchBriefs.emailSubject: AI-Projekte schneller zu produktionsreifer Vision-AI machen
researchBriefs.emailBody:
Hallo Herr/Frau [Name],

Viele Teams mit allgemeinem AI-Fokus sehen in Vision-Projekten denselben Engpass: Modellwahl, Optimierung und deploymentnahe Anpassungen ziehen sich oft länger als geplant.

ONE WARE automatisiert genau diesen Teil. Aus Ihren Daten entstehen in Minuten produktionsreife Vision-AI-Modelle, optimiert für die Zielhardware.

So lassen sich mehr Kundenprojekte mit dem gleichen Team liefern, bei besser planbaren Laufzeiten.

Wäre ein kurzer Austausch sinnvoll, um zu prüfen, ob das für Ihre laufenden Delivery-Projekte relevant ist?

Mit freundlichen Grüßen
[Ihr Name]
researchBriefs.phoneScript: Hallo Herr/Frau [Name], [Ihr Name] von ONE WARE. Wir sprechen mit AI-Dienstleistern, bei denen Vision-Projekte in Modellwahl und Optimierung zu viel Zeit kosten. Genau das automatisieren wir. Ist das bei Ihnen ein Thema?
researchBriefs.eventIdea: Check for presence at SPS, Automatica, Vision, or regional automation events.

researchBriefs.overview: gip automation GmbH is a German industrial automation and MES software provider specializing in planning, engineering, installation, commissioning, and support for industrial production and process industries. They deliver full automation solutions from field level to MES with ERP integration, focusing on batch/process automation in sectors such as chemicals, feed, building materials, and food. Their delivery model includes strong implementation ownership across the project lifecycle.
researchBriefs.qualificationSummary: gip automation GmbH is a strong candidate for ONE WARE's offering as they implement automation projects end-to-end and operate in industrial environments where Vision AI could accelerate project delivery and reduce trial and error. Their focus on MES and process automation suggests potential use cases for Vision AI models in quality control, process monitoring, or equipment inspection. The company likely faces challenges in speeding up model optimization and deployment cycles, which ONE WARE can address by automating production-ready Vision AI model creation, enabling faster, more predictable project timelines with smaller, hardware-efficient models.
qualifyingSignals: Full project lifecycle delivery including engineering, installation, commissioning, and support
Focus on industrial automation and MES software with integration to ERP systems
Specialization in batch/process automation with complex production steps
Strong implementation ownership indicating practical delivery challenges
Operating in Germany with German-speaking decision makers
riskFlags: Integrator-plus-software hybrid rather than pure software vendor
Focus on process and batch automation may limit discrete Vision AI use cases
No independent third-party validation of scale or revenue found
Potentially less aligned with discrete manufacturing Vision AI scenarios
researchBriefs.businessPotentialReasoning: Typical AI use cases start around 7,000 EUR, with complex production-grade deployments reaching 20,000 to 40,000 EUR. Given gip automation's full project delivery scope and industrial MES focus, initial Vision AI projects could realistically reach the upper mid-range. Potential for multiple use cases and recurring deployments exists but is moderated by their batch/process automation specialization.
researchBriefs.targetIndustry: Industrial Automation, Process Manufacturing, MES Software
researchBriefs.productsOffered: Automation technology, MES/SCADA/HMI/SPS software, proprietary MES systems (VarioBatch®, iPlus.MES), PLC/SCADA/HMI development
researchBriefs.personalizationRule: Personalize around gip automation's full project lifecycle delivery and potential bottlenecks in Vision AI model optimization and deployment speed within batch/process automation environments.

researchBriefs.overview: ZEUS Automation is a Germany-based industrial automation and consulting firm with a strong focus on embedded systems, software development, and AI-enabled industrial use cases. They deliver projects in regulated environments across automotive, aerospace, process industry, pharma, oil & gas, and manufacturing sectors. Their expertise includes PLC/SCADA/HMI integration, robotics, and compliance-heavy domains. ZEUS Automation appears to be primarily a service and integration provider rather than a product vendor, making them a relevant target for ONE WARE's software that accelerates production-ready Vision AI model creation.
researchBriefs.qualificationSummary: ZEUS Automation implements industrial automation projects with embedded AI components, indicating they face delivery challenges typical for integrators. Their broad service scope and focus on regulated industries suggest a need for predictable, efficient AI model deployment workflows. They likely encounter bottlenecks in vision AI model optimization and integration, which ONE WARE can address by automating model generation and hardware optimization, reducing trial and error and speeding time to production.
qualifyingSignals: Industrial automation integrator with embedded systems and AI-enabled use cases
Project delivery focus in regulated and compliance-heavy industries
Experience with PLC/SCADA/HMI and robotics integration
AI consulting and machine learning integration for industrial applications
International delivery experience indicating scalable project execution
riskFlags: Broad positioning across automation, software, IT security, and AI may dilute focus
No clear public evidence of product-led AI offerings; primarily service/integration-led
Marketing claims and project metrics not independently verified
No confirmed organization address on website, though Germany-based context is strong
researchBriefs.businessPotentialReasoning: As a general AI integrator with multiple industrial projects, ZEUS Automation could start with a single AI use case around 7,000 EUR and scale to more complex production-grade deployments worth 20,000 to 40,000 EUR. Given their broad industry coverage and international delivery, multiple use cases and repeat projects are likely, supporting a mid-range business potential estimate.
researchBriefs.targetIndustry: Industrial Automation, Automotive, Aerospace, Process Industry, Pharma, Oil & Gas, Manufacturing
researchBriefs.productsOffered: Embedded Systems, Software Development, PLC/SCADA/HMI Integration, Robotics, AI Consulting and Integration
researchBriefs.personalizationRule: Personalize outreach around ZEUS Automation's broad industrial automation delivery scope and likely bottlenecks in vision AI model optimization and integration within regulated environments.
researchBriefs.linkedInAngle: Frage nach dem größten Engpass bei Vision-AI-Projekten in der Modellgenerierung oder Integration, um den automatisierten Modellgenerierungsprozess von ONE WARE vorzustellen.
researchBriefs.emailAngle: Hervorhebung der typischen Engpässe bei Vision-AI-Projekten in der Modellwahl und Optimierung, die sich oft verzögern, und wie ONE WARE diese Schritte automatisiert, um schnell produktionsreife Modelle zu liefern.

researchBriefs.overview: ABAB Development e.U. is an Austrian embedded software development partner specializing in high-performance, reliable embedded solutions for automotive, rail, aerospace/defense, and industrial/medtech sectors. They offer full-lifecycle delivery from requirements engineering through software development, functional safety, firmware, AUTOSAR integration, and lifecycle management, using agile methods and modern CI/CD tooling. Their industry focus includes industrial automation and Industry 4.0 use cases, indicating relevant overlap with Vision AI deployment challenges in embedded contexts.
researchBriefs.qualificationSummary: ABAB Development e.U. is a strong candidate as an integrator partner for ONE WARE due to their embedded software delivery expertise in demanding industrial sectors and agile customer-centric approach. They implement projects (not just advisory), which aligns with ONE WARE’s value in accelerating production-ready Vision AI model delivery. Their focus on high-performance embedded systems and Industry 4.0 use cases suggests potential bottlenecks in model optimization and hardware-efficient deployment where ONE WARE’s automation can reduce trial and error and speed time to production.

researchBriefs.overview: Cegelec NDT-PSC is a France-based engineering and non-destructive testing (NDT) services company focused on industrial, aerospace, nuclear, and energy sectors. It operates primarily as a services-led integrator delivering inspections, interventions, process engineering, and training rather than as a product vendor. Their delivery ownership around complex inspection workflows and process qualification suggests potential fit for Vision AI automation that reduces trial and error and accelerates production readiness.

They likely face fixed-price and repeatable project pressures typical for integrators. Their industrial and nuclear customer base implies stringent production constraints and hardware realities. However, no direct evidence of internal AI model development or software product ownership was found, so ONE WARE’s value proposition should focus on accelerating model generation and integration efficiency within their existing inspection and process automation projects.

researchBriefs.linkedInMessage: Quick question: How fast can you currently get a Vision AI model production-ready for your inspection projects? We often see integrators spend unnecessary weeks on model choice and tuning. We automate exactly that part. How is it for you?
researchBriefs.emailSubject: Vision AI without long optimization phases
researchBriefs.emailBody:
Hello,

Are you currently working on Vision AI projects where it takes many iterations until a model is production-ready – or where it doesn’t reliably work despite multiple attempts?

We often see integrators spending weeks to months on tuning and deployment instead of focusing on the actual solution.

We developed software that creates custom Vision AI models in under 5 minutes, ready for production deployment. This enables teams to iterate much faster, plan project timelines better, and deliver more projects with the same team.

I’m curious: Where is your biggest bottleneck in Vision AI projects – model generation or integration?

Best regards,
[Your Name]
researchBriefs.phoneScript: Hello, this is [Your Name] from ONE WARE. I’ll keep it brief – do you have 30 seconds? We’re talking to integrators who spend weeks or months on model selection and optimization in Vision AI projects. We automate that part to speed up production readiness. How is it for you currently?
researchBriefs.eventIdea: Host a webinar or roundtable focused on accelerating Vision AI deployment in industrial inspection workflows, featuring case studies on reducing trial and error and improving project predictability for service-led integrators.

researchBriefs.overview: ALTEN Switzerland is an engineering and IT services firm focused on consulting and implementation across software, cloud, cybersecurity, embedded systems, quality assurance, and project management. It delivers client projects in regulated and industrial sectors with a strong emphasis on full lifecycle engineering and digital transformation. The company operates mainly as a service provider with a consultant-heavy staffing model and local Swiss offices.
researchBriefs.qualificationSummary: ALTEN Switzerland fits well as an implementation-led AI integrator with broad engineering and IT delivery capabilities. Their focus on embedded software and industrial sectors suggests they face typical Vision AI delivery bottlenecks such as model optimization and hardware constraints. They are not a product vendor but a service provider, making them a suitable partner for accelerating AI project delivery through automation of model generation and deployment. The company’s broad sector coverage and delivery orientation align with ONE WARE’s value proposition of faster, predictable, production-ready Vision AI models with lower engineering overhead.
